title: The Joneses Effect Is Stealing $6,200 From You
hook: Your neighbor's new car just triggered a spending cascade in your brain — and it costs the average American over six thousand dollars a year.
angle: Social comparison spending is biological, not a character flaw — but knowing the mechanism lets you flip it off like a switch.
audience: Anyone who has ever felt the urge to upgrade something after a friend or neighbor bought something nicer
key_numbers: 40% more likely to make a big purchase within weeks of a neighbor's purchase
